7 Months Postpartum

Reclaim Strength.
Rebuild Core.

Physiotherapist-designed · Anatomy-led · Postpartum safe

6Days/Week
400+Kcal/Session
60–90Min/Session
2Core Phases
6-Day Schedule

Weekly Reference Plan

Tap any exercise name to jump to its full guide.

⚠️
Before You Start

Get OB/GYN or pelvic floor physio clearance first. Watch midline during core work — coning or doming = stop, stay in Phase 1 only.

📈
Progressive Overload

Every 2 weeks: increase weight by smallest increment OR add 1–2 reps per set. Log every session. Day 7 = rest or 20–30 min easy walk only.

Session Start

Initial Cardio + Warm-Up

Do both blocks every session, in order.

Block 1 — Initial Cardio · 10 min

🎯 Purpose

Raise core temperature · prime cardiovascular system · mobilise joints before loading. Keep intensity LOW — 50–60% max HR only.

▶ Day-by-Day Initial Cardio Guide
10 min each dayLow Intensity · 50–60% HR
▼
Day 1 & 4
🚶 Treadmill Walk LISS
Speed 5.5–6 km/h · Incline 1–2% · Arms swing freely · No handrails
Day 2 & 5
🚴 Stationary Bike LISS
Light resistance (3–4) · 70–80 RPM · Upright posture · Relax shoulders
Day 3 & 6
🔄 Elliptical Steady
Resistance 3–4 · Natural stride · Use moving handles · Heel-drive
✓ All Days — Key Cues
  • This is warm-up, not training — keep it easy
  • Breathe through nose if possible
  • Gradually increase pace over first 3 minutes
  • Finish feeling warm, not tired
Block 2 — Dynamic Warm-Up · 8–10 min

🎯 Purpose

Activate glutes, core, and shoulder stabilisers before loaded exercises. Performed every session, same 4 movements.

▶ Bodyweight Squat
2 × 15No EquipmentGlute + Quad Activation
▼
▶ How
  • Feet shoulder-width · toes 10–15° out · hands at chest
  • Push hips back first, then bend knees · lower to parallel
  • Drive through heels · squeeze glutes at top
✓ Form Cues
  • Chest tall — never rounds fwd
  • Knees over 2nd toe always
  • Heels flat throughout
  • Exhale on the way UP
✕ Mistakes
  • Belly coning → reduce depth
  • Knees caving inward
  • Holding breath
→ Why
  • Restores glute–pelvic floor connection
  • Primes glutes + quads before loading
▶ Standing Marching Knee Raises
2 × 20 (10 each)No EquipmentHip Flexor Activation
▼
▶ How
  • Stand tall · light wall support optional
  • Lift knee to hip height · 1 sec hold · slow
  • Lower with control · alternate legs
✓ Form Cues
  • Pelvis level — no tilt on knee rise
  • Standing knee soft, not locked
  • Exhale as knee lifts
✕ Mistakes
  • Pelvis dropping → weak glute med
  • Kicking up fast → use slow control
  • Trunk leaning backward
→ Why
  • Re-activates inhibited hip flexors
  • Glute med challenge → protects knee + back
▶ Standing Hip Hinge (Bodyweight)
2 × 12No EquipmentPosterior Chain Prep
▼
▶ How
  • Feet hip-width · soft knees (stay fixed)
  • Push hips back · torso lowers fwd · spine straight
  • Drive hips fwd · squeeze glutes at top
✓ Form Cues
  • HINGE at hip — not lower back
  • Neutral spine head-to-tailbone
  • Weight in heels throughout
✕ Mistakes
  • Rounding lumbar → disc risk
  • Bending knees too much → squat
  • Hyperextending at top
→ Why
  • Foundation for all deadlift variations
  • Learn now = back protection all session
▶ Arm Swings + Cross-Body Shoulder Reach
2 × 15 eachNo EquipmentShoulder Mobility
▼
▶ How
  • Arms at shoulder height · swing across chest, then open wide
  • Cross-body: reach arm across · opposite hand presses above elbow · 2 sec hold
✓ Form Cues
  • Shoulders DOWN — away from ears
  • Smooth, controlled — not ballistic
  • Comfortable end-range only
✕ Mistakes
  • Shrugging → subacromial impingement
  • Moving too fast → no joint prep
→ Why
  • Counters rounded nursing posture
  • Warms rotator cuff before pressing + pulling
Strength Training

6-Day Split

Rest 60–90 sec between sets. Last 2 reps should feel challenging.

📐
Rule: Compound → then Isolation

Multi-joint lifts first (max calorie burn + hormonal response), isolation second (arm toning, aesthetics). Never increase weight if form breaks.

Day 1 — Upper Push
▶ Seated DB Shoulder Press
3 × 10–12Dumbbells · Bench 90°Compound
▼
▶ How
  • Bench 90° · back flat against pad · dumbbells at shoulder height, palms fwd
  • Elbows at 75° · exhale → press up to near-full extension
  • 3-sec slow descent back to start
✓ Cues
  • Back pressed to pad — no arch
  • Elbows slightly in front, not flared
  • Wrists neutral
  • Exhale on every press
✕ Avoid
  • Back arching off pad
  • Elbows flared 90° → impingement
  • Too heavy → form breaks
→ Why
  • Shoulder toning + width
  • Seated = back supported = postpartum safe
▶ Dumbbell Lateral Raise
3 × 12–15Dumbbells · StandingIsolation · Medial Delt
▼
▶ How
  • Stand · feet hip-width · dumbbells tilted fwd ("pouring water")
  • Exhale · raise to shoulder height · 1-sec pause
  • 3–4 sec slow descent — this is the toning work
✓ Cues
  • Lead with ELBOWS not hands
  • Shoulders DOWN throughout
  • Stop at shoulder height exactly
✕ Avoid
  • Shrugging traps → deltoid loses tension
  • Above shoulder → AC joint stress
  • Swinging torso
→ Why
  • Creates shoulder width + sculpture
  • Reverses postpartum forward-rounded posture
▶ Incline Dumbbell Press
3 × 10–12Dumbbells · Incline 30–45°Compound · Upper Chest
▼
▶ How
  • Bench 30–45° · scapulae retracted + depressed · elbows at 75°
  • Feet flat · exhale → press up to near-full extension
  • Lower slowly · full chest stretch at bottom · no bounce
✓ Cues
  • Wrists over elbows throughout
  • Natural lower back arch
  • Shoulder blades pinched and held down
✕ Avoid
  • Bench above 45° → shoulder takes over
  • Elbows flared 90° → pec tear risk
  • Bouncing at chest
→ Why
  • Builds chest → improves feeding posture
  • Incline = less lumbar arch than flat bench
▶ Cable Tricep Rope Pushdown
3 × 12–15Cable + Rope · High pulleyIsolation · Triceps
▼
▶ How
  • Cable high · rope attachment · elbows PINNED to ribs
  • Exhale → push down · split rope at bottom · full extension
  • 1-sec squeeze · return 3 sec to 70° bend
✓ Cues
  • Elbows glued to sides — the axis
  • Slight 10–15° fwd lean (not a full hinge)
  • Wrists straight throughout
✕ Avoid
  • Elbows drifting fwd → becomes shoulder
  • Bending at waist → back risk
  • Swinging for momentum
→ Why
  • Triceps = 60% of upper arm → fastest arm toner
  • Rope = wrist-neutral → joint-safe
Day 2 — Lower Body
▶ Leg Press Machine
4 × 12Leg Press MachineCompound · Quads + Glutes
▼
▶ How
  • Knees at 90° at start · feet shoulder-width · toes 10–15° out
  • Back pressed to seat ALWAYS · inhale lower 3 sec to 90°
  • Exhale · drive through heels · stop 2 inches before lock-out
✓ Cues
  • LOW BACK TO SEAT — #1 postpartum cue
  • Knees track over toes always
  • Drive through heels, not toes
✕ Avoid
  • Back peeling off seat → lumbar compression
  • Knees caving inward
  • Full lock-out → joints bearing load
→ Why
  • Safest high-load lower compound postpartum
  • Back fully supported → no spinal compression
▶ Dumbbell Romanian Deadlift
3 × 10Dumbbells · StandingCompound · Hamstrings
▼
▶ How
  • Feet hip-width · dumbbells at thighs · soft knee — does NOT move
  • Hips push BACK · dumbbells glide down legs · stop at hamstring stretch
  • Drive hips fwd · squeeze glutes hard at top
✓ Cues
  • HINGE not squat — knees barely move
  • Neutral spine throughout
  • Feel it in hamstrings — NOT back
✕ Avoid
  • Rounding lumbar → disc injury
  • Bending knees too much → becomes squat
  • Lowering past hamstring stretch
→ Why
  • Corrects anterior pelvic tilt (common postpartum)
  • Most effective posterior-chain developer
▶ Seated Leg Curl Machine
3 × 12–15Machine · SeatedIsolation · Hamstrings
▼
▶ How
  • Pad above ankle · thigh pad locked · sit tall
  • Exhale · curl through full range (90°+) · 1-sec squeeze
  • Return 3 sec slowly · don't let weight crash
✓ Cues
  • Hips pressed to seat throughout
  • Both legs curl symmetrically
  • Slow return = the toning phase
✕ Avoid
  • Hips lifting → lumbar compression
  • Momentum swinging
  • Partial reps
→ Why
  • Seated = no prone needed → safe if diastasis concern
  • Less lumbar compression than lying curl
▶ Cable Glute Kickback
3 × 15 each legCable + Ankle Cuff · LowIsolation · Glutes
▼
▶ How
  • Cable low · ankle cuff · hold frame · 15° fwd lean
  • Exhale · extend leg back · squeeze glute at end range
  • Stop at full hip extension — don't hyperextend back
✓ Cues
  • Movement from HIP — not lower back
  • Pelvis stays square — no rotation
  • Standing leg soft, not locked
✕ Avoid
  • Hyperextending back for more "kick"
  • Pelvis rotating
  • Too much weight → compensation
→ Why
  • Cable = constant tension full ROM
  • Glute strength → protects lumbar postpartum
▶ Dumbbell Walking Lunge (Day 5 only)
3 × 10 each legDumbbells · Open FloorCompound · Quads + Glutes
▼
▶ How
  • Dumbbells at sides · step forward into lunge · back knee toward floor
  • Front knee tracks over ankle · torso upright
  • Drive through front heel to step forward into next rep
✓ Cues
  • Chest tall throughout
  • Front knee never past toes
  • Core gently braced every step
✕ Avoid
  • Leaning forward → back knee drops with force
  • Front knee caving inward
  • Rushing through reps
→ Why
  • Progressive lower-body challenge on Day 5
  • Unilateral = fixes left-right strength imbalance
Day 3 — Upper Pull
▶ Lat Pulldown (Wide Overhand)
4 × 10–12Cable + Wide BarCompound · Lats + Biceps
▼
▶ How
  • Knees locked under pad · wide overhand grip · 15° lean back
  • DEPRESS scapulae first · THEN pull bar to upper chest
  • Elbows drive down + back · 3–4 sec controlled return
✓ Cues
  • "Shoulders DOWN then pull" — every rep
  • NEVER pull behind the neck
  • Control the ascent — no fly-up
✕ Avoid
  • Behind-neck pull → cervical injury
  • Excessive backward lean
  • Shrugging at top
→ Why
  • #1 postpartum back priority
  • Counters baby-carrying posture · prevents neck/shoulder pain
▶ Seated Cable Row (V-Bar)
3 × 12Cable + V-BarCompound · Mid-Back
▼
▶ How
  • V-bar · palms face each other · sit upright natural curve
  • Exhale · pull to navel · squeeze blades together
  • Return: full forward scapular stretch every rep
✓ Cues
  • Torso STILL — no rocking
  • Chest proud at full contraction
  • Full protraction at the front
✕ Avoid
  • Leaning back excessively
  • Rounding forward at start
  • Short partial reps
→ Why
  • V-bar neutral grip → wrist + elbow safe
  • Rhomboids = posture muscles → critical postpartum
▶ Dumbbell Bicep Curl
3 × 12–15Dumbbells · StandingIsolation · Biceps
▼
▶ How
  • Arms extended · palms up · elbows PINNED to sides
  • Exhale · curl up · wrists rotate out at top · hard squeeze
  • 3–4 sec slow descent to full extension
✓ Cues
  • Elbows fixed at sides — the axis
  • Full extension at bottom every rep
  • Wrists supinate fully at top
✕ Avoid
  • Elbows drifting fwd → front delt takes over
  • Swinging torso → reduce weight
  • Partial reps
→ Why
  • Slow eccentric = most important toning element
  • Pairs with tricep pushdown = complete arm
▶ Dumbbell Hammer Curl
2 × 12Dumbbells · StandingIsolation · Brachialis
▼
▶ How
  • Palms facing INWARD (thumbs up) · elbows pinned to sides
  • Exhale · curl up keeping wrists neutral · slow 3-sec descent
✓ Cues
  • Wrists stay neutral — no supination
  • Elbows fixed at sides
  • No torso swing
✕ Avoid
  • Wrists rotating (becomes standard curl)
  • Swinging to lift
→ Why
  • Targets brachialis → fuller arm look
  • Wrist-neutral = easier on joints
Postpartum Core

Two-Phase Core Protocol

After strength training. 10 min. Phase 1 for Weeks 1–3.

🚨
Coning Check — Every Session

Lie on back · lift head gently · watch midline between navel and sternum. See coning, doming, or ridging? → Stop that exercise. Stay in Phase 1. See pelvic floor physio.

📍 Phase 1 — Weeks 1–3

Rehabilitation + Activation

Re-link TVA · pelvic floor · diaphragm. 10 min total. All 4 exercises every session.

▶ Dead Bug
3 × 8 per sideMatTVA Activation
▼
▶ How
  • Lie on back · knees at 90° tabletop · arms up to ceiling
  • Inhale 3 counts · exhale → pelvic floor lift (stop-urination) + draw-in
  • WHILE exhaling: lower R arm + extend L leg · back stays FLAT
◉ Breathing
  • Exhale BEFORE and DURING movement
  • Never hold breath or bear down
  • Feel: deep quiet contraction between hip bones
  • NOT neck tension or outer obliques
✓ Cues
  • Lower back flat to mat — no gap, ever
  • Move S-L-O-W-L-Y
  • Inhale · return · repeat opposite side
✕ Avoid
  • Holding breath → IAP spike, dangerous
  • Back arching → reduce range
  • Coning/doming → STOP immediately
→ Why
  • Gold standard postpartum core — trains TVA without IAP spike
▶ Bird Dog
3 × 8 per sideMatSpinal Stabilisation
▼
▶ How
  • All-fours · knees under hips · hands under shoulders · back FLAT
  • Exhale + gentle brace · extend R arm + L leg to parallel
  • Hold 3 sec · squeeze glute · return controlled · switch
◉ Breathing
  • Exhale on extension · inhale on return
  • Feel: low ab + glute of extended leg + mild back burn
  • Test: water bottle on back — should not fall
✓ Cues
  • Hips LEVEL — no rotation when leg extends
  • Neck in line with spine — look at floor
✕ Avoid
  • Hip rotation → stabilisers not activating
  • Hyperextending lower back
  • Moving fast for reps
▶ Glute Bridge
3 × 15MatGlute + Pelvic Floor
▼
▶ How
  • Lie back · knees 90° · feet flat hip-width · arms flat
  • Inhale · exhale → lift pelvic floor · drive through heels · lift hips
  • Hold 2 sec · squeeze glutes · lower vertebra-by-vertebra
◉ Breathing
  • Pelvic floor LIFTS on way up · RELAXES on way down
  • Feel: glutes burning (not just hamstrings)
  • Tip: feet too far? Bring closer to glutes
✓ Cues
  • Ribs DOWN — no flaring at top
  • No hyperextension at top
  • Chin tucked throughout
✕ Avoid
  • Hyperextending lumbar at top
  • Thrusting with momentum
  • Bearing DOWN through pelvic floor on lift
▶ Heel Slides
3 × 10 per sideMat · Smooth FloorTVA Isolation
▼
▶ How
  • Lie back · knees bent · fingertips 2 inches inside hip bones
  • Inhale · exhale → gentle TVA draw-in (20–30% effort)
  • Slide R heel slowly · lower back must NOT change position
◉ Breathing
  • Breathe continuously — never hold
  • Feel: deep quiet contraction between hip bones
  • Back arching? → shorten the slide
✓ Cues
  • Back position unchanged during slide
  • Gentle 20–30% — not a hard brace
  • Move slowly and deliberately
✕ Avoid
  • Back lifting → TVA disengaged · shorten range
  • Using outer obliques instead of TVA
  • Moving too fast
📍 Phase 2 — After Week 3

Progression: Load + Anti-Rotation

Ready when: 15 perfect Dead Bugs + Bird Dogs · no coning · no pelvic floor symptoms · stable 30-sec glute bridge hold.

▶ Side Plank (Modified — Knee)
3 × 20–30 sec per sideMatLateral Core
▼
▶ How
  • Lie on right side · knees bent 90° · elbow under shoulder
  • Exhale · lift hips → straight diagonal line · hold 20–30 sec
  • Breathe continuously · lower · 30 sec rest · switch
✓ Cues
  • Hips lifted — no sagging
  • Body in ONE straight line
  • Shoulder packed — push floor away
  • NEVER hold breath
✕ Avoid
  • Hips dropping → reduce hold time
  • Shoulder collapsing into neck
→ Why
  • Lateral stability → safe heavy lifting
  • No spinal compression
▶ Glute Bridge March
3 × 10 per legMatPelvic Stability
▼
▶ How
  • Hold glute bridge at top · hips up · straight line
  • Exhale · lift R knee toward chest · hold 2 sec
  • Pelvis does NOT drop or rotate · lower · switch legs
✓ Cues
  • Hips LEVEL — that is the whole challenge
  • Squeeze standing-leg glute hard
  • Exhale on knee raise
✕ Avoid
  • Hips dropping → reduce hold time
  • Pelvis rotating
→ Why
  • Mimics walking, stairs, carrying baby
  • Trains glute med + TVA + pelvic floor together
▶ Pallof Press (Cable Anti-Rotation)
3 × 10 per sideCable + D-HandleAnti-Rotation
▼
▶ How
  • Cable mid-chest · D-handle · stand sideways 3 ft away · feet shoulder-width
  • Both hands at chest · exhale → press straight out until fully extended
  • RESIST cable pulling you · hold 2 sec · return · all reps one side then switch
✓ Cues
  • Torso SQUARE facing fwd — whole exercise
  • Feet planted · exhale on press
✕ Avoid
  • Rotating toward machine → defeats purpose
  • Too much weight → feel core not arms
→ Why
  • Best anti-rotation without bands
  • Mimics baby-carrying, asymmetric daily loads
▶ Forearm Plank (from Knees)
3 × 20–30 secMatCore Integration
▼
▶ How
  • Forearms on floor · elbows under shoulders · knees on mat
  • Body straight line hip to head · gentle brace (30%) · squeeze glutes
  • Breathe continuously · hold · progress to toes when no coning
✓ Cues
  • Hips level — no pike or sag
  • Neck in line with spine
  • NEVER hold breath during hold
✕ Avoid
  • Hips hiking up
  • Lower back sagging
  • Holding breath → IAP spike
→ Why
  • Integrates TVA + glutes + shoulder stabilisers
  • Knee version = reduced connective tissue load
Session Finish

Post-Workout Cardio + Cool-Down

Do both blocks after every session, in order. Never skip cool-down.

Block 1 — Fat-Loss Cardio
🔬
Why Post-Strength Cardio Burns More Fat

Strength depletes glycogen → body taps fat stores 25–40% sooner. Intervals create EPOC: metabolism elevated 12–36 hrs after session.

▶ Day-by-Day Fat-Loss Cardio Guide
15–20 min per day65–80% Max HR
▼
Day 1 & 4
🏔️ Treadmill Incline Walk LISS
20 min · Incline 8–12% · Speed 5.5–6.5 km/h · No handrails · Target 65–72% HR · Walk tall, don't lean
Day 2 & 5
🚴 Cycling Intervals HIIT
20 min · Warm 3 min (R3–4) · 6 rounds: 90s hard (R7–8, 80–90 RPM) + 90s easy (R3) · Cool 2 min
Day 3 & 6
🔄 Elliptical Steady State Steady
15–20 min · Resistance 4–6 · Target 70–80% HR · Active arms (push + pull) · Heel-drive each stride
✓ Intensity Check
  • LISS/Steady: can hold full conversation
  • HIIT hard: only 2–3 words between breaths
  • HIIT recovery: short conversation ok
→ Fat-Loss Logic
  • LISS: 250–320 kcal · zero joint impact
  • HIIT: EPOC keeps metabolism high 12–36h
  • All: zero pelvic floor pressure (no running)
Block 2 — Cool-Down Stretching · 8–10 min
🌿
Non-Negotiable Postpartum

Relaxin keeps joints lax during breastfeeding. Tight muscles on lax joints = chronic pain. This is your daily injury insurance — never skip.

▶ Standing Hamstring Stretch
30–45 sec per side · 2 roundsHamstrings
▼
Heel on bench · leg straight Flex foot — toes pointing up Hinge fwd from HIPS (not waist) Back stays straight throughout Each exhale → deepen lean slightly Don't round the lower back
💡 Feel: entire back of thigh
▶ Standing Quad Stretch
30–45 sec per side · 2 roundsQuads · Hip Flexors
▼
One hand on wall for balance Grip top of foot (not ankle) Push hip FORWARD to deepen Knees level — bent knee points down Stand tall — no forward lean Don't arch back to pull foot higher
💡 Feel: front thigh from knee to hip
▶ Calf Stretch — Gastroc + Soleus
30 sec each variation per sideCalves · Achilles
▼
Back foot 2 ft from wall · hands on wall Gastroc: back leg STRAIGHT · heel flat · lean in Soleus: same position + BEND back knee slightly Heel stays flat throughout Each exhale → lean slightly closer
💡 Straight = upper calf · Bent knee = Achilles
▶ Doorway Chest Stretch
40 sec per side · 2 roundsPecs · Anterior Deltoid
▼
Forearm flat on wall · elbow at 90° shoulder height Step one foot fwd · rotate torso AWAY from wall Elbow at shoulder height EXACTLY (not higher) Each exhale → more chest opening Don't lean body fwd — torso upright
💡 Feel: chest + front shoulder
▶ Cross-Body Shoulder Stretch
30–40 sec per side · 2 roundsPosterior Deltoid
▼
Arm across body at shoulder height Opposite hand presses ABOVE elbow (not on joint) Shoulder DOWN and BACK — no shrug Turn head away for extra trap stretch
💡 Feel: back of shoulder + outer arm
▶ Child's Pose — Lower Back Release
60–90 sec totalErector Spinae · QL · Hips
▼
Kneel · sit back toward heels Arms extended fwd · palms flat · forehead to mat Walk hands RIGHT 20 sec · then LEFT 20 sec Each exhale → feel lower back expand Neck fully relaxed throughout
💡 Feel: entire lower back lengthening · hips opening
POSTPARTUM STRENGTH & FAT LOSS
7 Months Postpartum · Physiotherapy + Anatomy-Based · Always confirm medical clearance before starting
